name: CLI Foundation
description: Scaffold the CLI app's entry point, command dispatcher, shared visual identity module, and the subsystems every command needs: flow loader, argv-to-Zod input parser, exit-code mapper, pre-run banner, final summary, and the TTY progress display. No user-facing commands are implemented yet — those come in Sprint 7 — but this sprint delivers the infrastructure and visual grammar all of them will sit on. All visual output must conform to `relay-product_spec.md` §4 (voice), §5 (mark), §6 (CLI UX), §11 (progress display) — no emojis, Unicode only, specific symbol vocabulary.

waves.name: CLI entry point and bin shim
waves.description: Populate `packages/cli/src/cli.ts` with the ESM entry point: a `main()` that parses argv, instantiates the dispatcher (task_43), and routes based on argv[0]. Handle top-level errors: catch any thrown PipelineError, map to exit code (task_46 will provide the mapping; for now just log and exit 1 as placeholder — wired to real mapper in wave 2). Populate `packages/cli/bin/relay.js` shebang `#!/usr/bin/env node` that imports `../dist/cli.js` and calls `main()`. Set executable bit hint in package.json via shebang. Handle `--version` at the top level — output MUST match product spec §6.10 format: `●─▶●─▶●─▶●  relay <ver>`, then indented continuation lines `@relay/cli <ver>`, `@relay/core <ver>`, `node <ver> · claude <ver>`. Four lines total — optimized for easy paste into GitHub issues. Defer the mark constant to task_77's visual module (this task can stub with a string literal; task_77 wires the real import in Sprint 6 wave 2).

waves.name: Command dispatcher
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/dispatcher.ts` exporting `buildProgram(): Command` using `commander`. Register all v1 commands as stubs (each delegates to `src/commands/<name>.ts` via dynamic import — the real handlers are built in Sprint 7 and later). Commands: `list`, `search <query>`, `install <flow>`, `run <flow> [input...]`, `resume <runId>`, `runs`, `upgrade [flow]`, `doctor`, `new <name>`, `publish <path>`, `test <path>`, `logs <runId>`, `config`. Add the §5.2.1 shorthand: if the first positional is NOT one of the above commands AND matches an installed flow name OR is a path, route to `run`. Global options: `--verbose`, `--run-dir <path>`, `--no-color` (respect NO_COLOR env per product spec §17 #2). Return the Command for `cli.ts` to call `.parseAsync(argv)`. When no command + no args, print the splash help (implemented in Sprint 12 task_83).

waves.name: Visual identity module
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/visual.ts` — the single source of truth for the Relay brand grammar per product spec §4 and §5. Export: (a) `MARK = '●─▶●─▶●─▶●'` constant (the signature); (b) `WORDMARK = '●─▶●─▶●─▶●  relay'`; (c) `SYMBOLS = { ok:'✓', fail:'✕', warn:'⚠', spinner: ['⠋','⠙','⠹','⠸','⠼','⠴','⠦','⠧','⠇','⠏'], pending:'○', dot:'·', arrow:'▶', cancelled:'⊘' }`; (d) color helpers using `chalk` honoring `NO_COLOR` + `--no-color` + non-TTY: `green(s)`, `yellow(s)`, `red(s)`, `gray(s)`, `bold(s)` that pass through raw string when colors are disabled; (e) layout helpers `rule(width=80)` returning a horizontal rule, `header(text)`, `footer(text)`, `kvLine(key, value)` for the aligned key-value layout used by the banner; (f) voice-compliance note in a doc comment citing product spec §4.1–§4.2 (no emojis, no exclamation marks, no 'simply', numbers over adjectives). Throw if anyone imports emoji from here. This module is the ONLY place the mark and symbols are defined — every command imports from here.

waves.name: Flow loader
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/flow-loader.ts` exporting `loadFlow(nameOrPath: string, cwd: string): Promise<{ flow: Flow; dir: string; pkg: any }>` implementing the §5.3.1 resolution order: (1) if looks like a path (starts with ./,../,/ or contains /), resolve to absolute and import `<dir>/dist/flow.js`; (2) check `<cwd>/.relay/flows/<name>/`; (3) check `<cwd>/node_modules/@ganderbite/flow-<name>/`; (4) throw an error instructing the user to `relay install <name>`. In all resolved cases, dynamic-import the flow's default export (assert it's a Flow via duck-typing — has name, steps, graph). Read package.json for the `relay` metadata block. Return { flow, dir, pkg }. Log which source was resolved.

waves.name: Argv to Zod input parser
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/input-parser.ts` exporting `parseInputFromArgv(schema: ZodSchema, args: string[]): unknown`. Implementation: introspect the Zod schema (must be ZodObject) to discover field names + types + defaults. For each arg: if `--key=value` or `--key value`, map to the named field; positionals fill required fields in declared order (first positional → first required field of string/path type). Coerce numbers/booleans based on schema. Run .parse() at end; on failure throw FlowDefinitionError with field-level issues. Export `renderHelpFromSchema(schema): string` that produces human-readable usage text derived from the Zod schema's .describe() metadata where present. Used by `run` and `new` commands.

waves.name: Exit code mapper
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/exit-codes.ts` exporting `exitCodeFor(err: unknown): number` per §8.2: StepFailureError → 1, FlowDefinitionError → 2, ClaudeAuthError / ProviderAuthError → 3, HandoffSchemaError → 4, TimeoutError → 5, other PipelineError → 1, generic Error → 1. Also export `formatError(err)` that produces output matching product spec §12 template EXACTLY: `✕ <headline>` red, blank line, two-space-indented plain-English explanation, blank line, two-space-indented `→ <command>` lines (one per remediation). Known shapes: ClaudeAuthError with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY → produces the §12 three-line remediation (unset / --api-key / relay doctor); FlowDefinitionError with cycle → names the cycle path explicitly (§12 example); HandoffSchemaError → prints the invalid field, expected vs got, and `relay logs <runId> --step <name>` + `edit prompts/<file>` + `relay resume <runId>` remediations; TimeoutError → points at `.partial` artifact path + timeoutMs edit + resume; ClaudeAuthError 'binary missing' → links to install page. Every error MUST name a next command — no dead-ends. Use visuals from task_77.

waves.name: Pre-run banner, success banner, failure banner
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/banner.ts` exporting three functions, each producing output that matches the product spec VERBATIM. Import MARK/SYMBOLS/color helpers from task_77's `visual.ts` — do not redefine. (1) `renderStartBanner({ flow, runId, auth, input, costEstimate?, stepCount, etaMin })` — produces the block from product spec §6.3: first line `●─▶●─▶●─▶●  relay`, blank line, then kv rows `flow     <name> v<ver>`, `input    <primary> (<extras>)`, `run      <runId>  ·  <ISO date>`, `bill     <billingSource> (<detail>)  ·  <qualifier>` (e.g. 'no api charges' for subscription, 'billing api account' otherwise), `est      $<cost>  ·  <N> steps  ·  ~<min> min`, blank line, gray line `press ctrl-c any time — state is saved after every step.`, rule line. The `bill` row is NEVER silent. (2) `renderSuccessBanner(result, auth)` — produces §6.5 format: `●─▶●─▶●─▶●  <flowName> · <runId>  ✓`, per-step lines `<status> <name padded> <model>  <duration>  $<cost>`, summary `all N steps succeeded in <duration>`, kv rows for `cost` (label 'estimated api equivalent; billed to subscription' when billingSource==subscription, 'billed to api account' otherwise) and `output` (artifact path), then a `next:` block listing 'open the report', 'run again fresh', 'share with team (coming v1.1)'. (3) `renderFailureBanner(result, auth, err)` — produces §6.6 format: same header with `✕`, per-step lines with the failing step expanded to two indented lines naming the error class + the specific field/handoff, then `N of M steps succeeded · $<spent> spent · state saved`, blank line, `to resume after fixing:`, indented `relay resume <runId>`, blank line, `to restart from scratch:`, indented `relay run <flow> . --fresh`, blank line, `to inspect:`, indented `relay logs <runId>` and a `cat ./.relay/runs/<runId>/handoffs/<id>.json` hint when the error names a handoff. Every banner function must auto-disable color via task_77's helpers.

waves.name: TTY progress display
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/progress.ts` exporting `ProgressDisplay` class. MUST match product spec §6.4 + §11 layout exactly — three zones (fixed header, step grid, fixed footer) per §11.2. Constructor accepts `(runDir, flow, auth)`. Methods: `start()` watches `<runDir>/live/*.json` (fs.watch + 100ms debounce + poll fallback) and rewrites the single-screen layout using ANSI cursor sequences. Import MARK/SYMBOLS/color helpers from task_77. Header line: `●─▶●─▶●─▶●  <flowName> · <runId>` — static during run. Step row format per §11.3 (column-aligned so eye scans diagonally): `<sym> <stepName padded> <model padded> <progress> <tokensIn>K→<tokensOut>K    ~$<costLive>` where sym is SYMBOLS.pending '○' for pending, SYMBOLS.spinner rotator for running, SYMBOLS.ok '✓' for succeeded, SYMBOLS.fail '✕' for failed; progress is '<duration>s' for completed or 'turn N' or 'waiting on X, Y' (not 'pending' — explicit about why it's waiting, per §6.4). Cost uses `~` prefix for in-flight. Footer is TWO lines: totals `est  $<est>    spent  $<spent>    elapsed  <HH:MM>    ctrl-c saves state` + empty continuation line. Per §11.4 do NOT show: raw Claude output, progress bars, percentage-complete, celebrations. On ctrl-c, transition to 'paused' display (§11.5) handled by task_87 in Sprint 12 — this task writes a hook for the handler. Non-TTY fallback per §6.4: one structured line per state transition, e.g. `<ISO> info step.start stepId=inventory model=sonnet`. `stop()` clears the screen and returns control to the banner renderer (task_47).
